calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Cost per outcome calculation is not available. This organisation conducts significant multi-domain work (e.g., human education, environmental conservation). Dividing the total budget solely by quantified animal outcomes would artificially inflate the cost and misrepresent their financial efficiency.

details.formula: Calculation aborted due to multi-domain operations.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: A quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'Leveraging the expertise of 24 local ecologists, this comprehensive assessment evaluated 886 terrestrial and freshwater species across eight animal groups. Alarmingly, the report revealed that 21 of the 886 assessed species have already disappeared from Hong Kong entirely, and over a quarter (232 out of 886) are at risk of local extinction.'
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.
Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: Organisation does not engage in direct animal sheltering; metric not applicable.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: ADMINISTRATION HK$4.3M
SUPPORTER BASE DEVELOPMENT HK$16.1M
CONSERVATION HK$57.3M
COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT AND EDUCATION HK$24.9M
WWF NETWORK HK$4.1M
CONSERVATION HK$57.3M, COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT AND EDUCATION HK$24.9M, WWF NETWORK HK$4.1M
Total expenditure for FY25 amounted to HK$106.7 million.
INDIVIDUAL DONATIONS HK$53.2M, CORPORATE DONATIONS HK$9.0M
PUBLIC SECTOR GRANTS HK$15.1M
WWF-Hong Kong's income decreased by 2% to HK$106.8 million in FY25.
Reviving Our Corals has nurtured 680 coral fragments in the Hoi Ha Marine Life Centre nursery. These have been successfully outplanted to sites in the Tolo Harbour and Channel, and 85% have survived.

In an era defined by urgent environmental challenges, WWF-Hong Kong's unwavering commitment to preserving our city's rich natural heritage is more important than ever. The conservation of biodiversity and ensuring ecosystem resilience demand visionary leadership, cross-sector collaboration and unyielding, collective action. We proudly embrace our responsibility, prioritising the needs of nature and mobilising a broad coalition of stakeholders to create lasting benefits for society and the environment and secure a thriving future for Hong Kong.

Leveraging the expertise of 24 local ecologists, this comprehensive assessment evaluated 886 terrestrial and freshwater species across eight animal groups. Alarmingly, the report revealed that 21 of the 886 assessed species have already disappeared from Hong Kong entirely, and over a quarter (232 out of 886) are at risk of local extinction.

metrics.metric_name: Species at risk of local extinction

source.quote: Leveraging the expertise of 24 local ecologists, this comprehensive assessment evaluated 886 terrestrial and freshwater species across eight animal groups. Alarmingly, the report revealed that 21 of the 886 assessed species have already disappeared from Hong Kong entirely, and over a quarter (232 out of 886) are at risk of local extinction.
